ogTitle: Hardwicke Bay vs Hardy: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hardwicke Bay and Hardy.
heading: Hardwicke Bay vs Hardy — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hardwicke Bay, South Australia and Hardy, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hardy has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Hardwicke Bay (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hardwicke Bay or Hardy safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hardwicke Bay has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Hardy scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hardwicke Bay scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Hardy scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hardwicke Bay or Hardy?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hardy has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
